news.summary: In the city of Thousand Oaks, California, a company reported its first quarter highlights for 2025. The average production for the first quarter was 4,077 barrels of oil equivalent per day (BOEPD), marking a 23% rise from the previous year's average of 3,305 BOEPD. This increase was attributed to additional production from wells drilled and completed in 2024. The net income for the same period was $5.8 million, showing a significant 72% increase compared to the first quarter of the previous year.
news.headline: Kolibri Global Energy Inc. reports 72% increase in Q1 2025 net income to $5.8 million, with average production of 4,077 BOEPD.

news.summary: A recent report by Descartes Systems Group revealed insights on the changing trends in ecommerce home delivery, particularly among younger consumers. The study, titled "How Smarter Home Delivery Wins Younger Consumers as Online Buying Slows," focused on consumer sentiment and purchasing behaviors. It highlighted that consumers aged 18-35, referred to as "under 35s," played a significant role in driving online growth. While the overall ecommerce market saw slower growth, under 35s increased both the volume and frequency of their online purchases over the past year. The report indicated that 43% of under 35s ramped up their spending, in contrast to 18% of all consumers who reduced their purchases during the same period. This trend was further emphasized by the fact that only 32% of individuals over 65 increased their online spending year-on-year. The study sheds light on the importance of catering to younger consumers and adapting delivery strategies to meet their evolving needs in the changing ecommerce landscape.
news.headline: Descartes study finds young consumers driving online purchases, with 79% facing delivery issues. Under 35s grew spending by 43%.

news.summary: Affimed N.V., a company focusing on immuno-oncology and listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol AFMD, has recently received notification from the Nasdaq Listing Qualifications Department regarding the suspension of trading for its common shares. In accordance with Nasdaq rules, trading will cease on May 20, 2025, and a delisting notice will be filed with the U.S. Securities Exchange Commission. This action is being taken under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5101, 5110(b), and IM-5101-1. Affimed N.V. aims to empower cancer patients by enhancing their natural ability to combat the disease.
news.headline: Affimed N.V. receives delisting notice from Nasdaq, trading to be suspended on May 20, 2025. Securities to be delisted.

news.summary: Kazakhstan aims to become a prominent crypto hub in Central Asia by relaxing regulatory restrictions, as suggested by Kanysh Tuleushin, the country's first vice minister of digital development. Tuleushin believes that digital mining and policy shifts could establish Kazakhstan as a leader in blockchain technology. He emphasizes the potential impact of lifting restrictions, citing the potential addition of hundreds of billions of tenge to the national budget. Tuleushin advocates for broader legalization, taxation, and implementation of clear crypto regulations, transparent exchanges, and legal crypto ATMs. Binance's CZ signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Kazakhstan in 2023, showing external interest in the nation's crypto potential. Furthermore, crypto mining companies are seen as potential drivers of infrastructure modernization in Kazakhstan, similar to their role in the US in balancing power grids by consuming surplus energy. Tuleushin's 70/30 energy initiative suggests involving foreign investors in upgrading thermal power plants, with the generated capacity being split 70%-30% between the national grid and miners, respectively. He also proposes utilizing associated petroleum gas to power data centers, reducing emissions and creating revenue for oil producers. The country's crypto mining sector has already contributed $34.6 million in taxes over three years, with 415,000 mining machines registered and licenses issued for mining pools. The AIFC exchange saw a surge in crypto trading from $324 million in 2023 to $1.4 billion in 2024, with plans for miners to sell 75% of their assets through AIFC platforms beginning in 2025.Despite these advancements, unregulated crypto transactions persist outside the AIFC, with an estimated $4.1 billion turnover in 2023, mostly beyond government oversight. In 2024, 36 illegal exchanges were shut down, freezing $4.8 million in assets and disrupting two Ponzi schemes. Additionally, Kazakhstan is developing a central bank digital currency, the digital tenge, with plans initially set for launch in 2025. Fellow Central Asian countries Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan are also adopting friendly approaches toward the digital asset industry. Binance signed an MOU with Kyrgyzstan's National Agency for Investments in May 2023 to introduce crypto payment infrastructure and blockchain education in the country.
news.headline: Kazakhstan aims to be Central Asia's top crypto hub with eased regulations, as mining tax revenue hits $34.6M in 3 years. Source: CZ #crypto

news.summary: Major financial firms have revised their U.S. recession predictions following a 90-day tariff pause between the U.S. and China. According to recent reports, Goldman Sachs has decreased the likelihood of a recession from 45% to 35%, while Barclays has completely ruled out the possibility. J.P. Morgan now estimates the chances to be less than 50%. This shift in forecasts occurred after both countries agreed to lower tariffs - the U.S. reduced duties on Chinese imports from 145% to 30%, and China lowered rates on U.S. goods from 125% to 10%. In addition, Goldman has raised its GDP forecast for 2025 by 0.5 points to 1%. For more details, refer to the full story on the Benzinga website.
news.headline: Major brokerages like Goldman Sachs, Barclays, and J.P. Morgan retract US recession forecasts post 90-day US-China tariff truce. Odds of U.S. recession reduced: Goldman Sachs down to 35%, Barclays rules out a recession, J.P. Morgan predicts less than 50%. Revised forecasts due to tariff reductions: US duties on Chinese imports down from 145% to 30%, China's rates on US goods lowered from 125% to 10%. Goldman raises 2025 GDP forecast by 0.5 points to 1%. Less Fed cuts expected.

This innovative tool incorporates artificial intelligence to facilitate job matching, offer career resources, deliver automated job notifications, and enable easy access to the membership portal. news.summary: The SEC has postponed its decision on the proposed spot Solana ETF to October 2025, along with similar decisions on other cryptocurrencies like Litecoin, Polkadot, XRP, and Dogecoin. The introduction of spot ETFs like the US spot Bitcoin ETFs, which captured 75% of new investments after launching, plays a significant role in enhancing liquidity and institutional adoption of digital assets. Despite delays, investors remain positive about the approval of a SOL ETF before the end of 2025, with an 82% approval chance predicted. Other crypto ETF applications for Polkadot, XRP, and Dogecoin are approaching SEC deadlines in June, with decisions expected around the 11th, 17th, and 24th. The SEC has a history of utilizing its full 240-day review period for evaluating crypto-related financial products.
news.headline: SEC postpones Solana ETF decision to Oct 2025. Polkadot, XRP ETFs await SEC ruling in June. Investors optimistic about ETF approval.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures surged over 2% in premarket trading following the announcement of a 90-day truce between the U.S. and China, reducing reciprocal tariffs from 125% to 10%. Trade negotiations over the weekend in Geneva showed progress, with the U.S. maintaining 20% tariffs on Chinese imports related to fentanyl, keeping the overall tariff level at 30%. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.44%, while the two-year bond was at 3.98%. CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 92.1% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in June. Key futures changes include Dow Jones (+2.12%), S&P 500 (+2.76%), Nasdaq 100 (+3.68%), and Russell 2000 (+3.16%). ETFs tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 also showed increase in premarket trading. Notable sector movements and specific company performances were seen on Friday. EchoStar Corp. saw a rise in sales while Plains All American Pipeline LP reported a decline in revenue. The Dow Jones index closed at 41,249.38, down 119 points or 0.29%.
news.headline: US stock futures rise over 2% after US and China agree to 90-day tariff pause, reducing tariffs from 125% to 10%. Treasury bonds yields at 4.44% and 3.98%. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged in June. Dow Jones up 2.12%, S&P 500 up 2.76%, Nasdaq 100 up 3.68%, Russell 2000 up 3.16%. SPY up 2.74% to $579.80, QQQ up 3.81% to $506.54. Energy, real estate, consumer discretionary, and materials sectors advanced, while health care, communication services, consumer staples, and information technology sectors declined. EchoStar Corp. rises 1.43%, Plains All American Pipeline LP falls nearly 3%.
